back: 応力 (Stress) carried by soil skeleton. Controls せん断強さ (shear strength), 圧密 (consolidation), and 液状化 (liquefaction). σ = 全応力 (total stress), u = 間隙水圧 (pore water pressure). Foundation of all geotechnical analysis (テルツァーギ (Terzaghi) 1925).
モール・クーロン (Mohr-Coulomb) failure criterion. c' = effective 粘着力 (cohesion) (soil bonding). φ' = 摩擦角 (friction angle) (particle interlocking). Both depend on drainage conditions. CD/CU/UU tests in lab.
Time-dependent compression of saturated 粘土 (clay) as excess pore pressure dissipates. Tv = time factor. cv = coefficient of 圧密 (consolidation). H = drainage path. Longer drainage = more time.

Saturated loose 砂 (sand) loses strength when σ' → 0 during 地震 (earthquake). FL < 1.0 = liquefiable. CSR = cyclic 応力 (stress) ratio from 地震 (earthquake). CRR = cyclic resistance ratio from N値 (N-value) or Vs.
Volume of voids / volume of solids. Loose 砂 (sand): e ≈ 0.7-0.9. Dense 砂 (sand): e ≈ 0.4-0.6. Related to porosity: n = e/(1+e). Decreases during 圧密 (consolidation).
State of compaction relative to loosest and densest states. Dr < 35% = loose. 35-65% = medium. 65-85% = dense. >85% = very dense. Correlated to 標準貫入試験 (SPT) N値 (N-value) and コーン貫入試験 (CPT) qc.
Rate of water flow through soil per unit area per unit hydraulic gradient. 粘土 (Clay): k = 10⁻⁸-10⁻¹⁰ m/s. シルト (Silt): 10⁻⁶-10⁻⁸. 砂 (Sand): 10⁻³-10⁻⁵. 礫 (Gravel): >10⁻³.
テルツァーギ (Terzaghi)'s general 支持力 (bearing capacity) equation. Nc, Nq, Nγ = dimensionless factors depending on φ'. q = surcharge = γDf. Allowable: qa = qu/FS (FS=3 typical).

Primary 圧密 (consolidation) 沈下 (settlement). Cc = compression index. H = layer thickness. e0 = initial 間隙比 (void ratio). σ'f = final 有効応力 (effective stress). σ'0 = initial. Use Cs for unloading/reloading.
Uniaxial compression of cohesive soil. Undrained せん断強さ (shear strength) cu = qu/2. Used for soft 粘土 (clay) classification. qu < 25 kPa = very soft. 25-50 = soft. 50-100 = medium. >200 = hard.
Primary wave. Particle motion parallel to propagation. Travels in solids and liquids. Typical in soil: 200-2000 m/s. Saturated soil dominated by water: Vp ≈ 1500 m/s. Used in refraction surveys.

Secondary wave. Particle motion perpendicular to propagation. Cannot travel through liquids. Key for Vs30 (Vs30), site classification, and dynamic 弾性係数 (modulus). G = ρVs² (small ひずみ (strain) shear 弾性係数 (modulus)).
Time-average Vs in top 30m. Used for seismic site classification. Japan: 告示1457号. Class A: Vs30 (Vs30) > 600 m/s. Class B/C: 150-600. Class D (軟弱地盤): < 150 m/s.
Surface wave. Retrograde elliptical particle motion. Dispersive in layered media. Velocity depends on frequency (wavelength). Longer wavelengths sample deeper layers. Used in 表面波探査 (MASW) surveys.

Dynamic (small-ひずみ (strain)) moduli from seismic velocities. G = shear 弾性係数 (modulus). E = Young's 弾性係数 (modulus). ν = Poisson's ratio. For saturated soil: ν → 0.5 (undrained). Dry 砂 (sand): ν ≈ 0.25-0.35.
Wave refraction at interface. At critical angle ic = arcsin(V₁/V₂), wave travels along interface as head wave. ic = arcsin(V₁/V₂). Used in 屈折法 (seismic refraction) to find layer depths.
63.5 kg hammer, 760 mm drop. N = blows per 300 mm. Energy correction to 60% (N60). Overburden correction: (N1)60 = CN·N60. JIS A 1219. Japanese ER ≈ 72% (auto hammer).
Continuous profile of qc, fs, u2 at 20 mm/s. Qt = normalized tip resistance. Fr = friction ratio (%). Ic = soil behavior type index. Ic < 2.05: 砂 (sand). Ic > 2.95: 粘土 (clay).

ボーリング孔 (Borehole) seismic method measuring Vp and Vs at depth intervals. Surface source (hammer), downhole receiver. JGS 3511. Used for Vs30 (Vs30), dynamic moduli, seismic design. More accurate than surface 表面波探査 (MASW).

jp: プレッシャーメーター試験
en: プレッシャーメーター試験
back: In-situ lateral 応力 (stress)-ひずみ (strain) measurement. Inflatable probe in ボーリング孔 (borehole). Ep = プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter) 弾性係数 (modulus). PL = limit pressure (ultimate). Useful in soft 岩 (rock), stiff clays. Ménard プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter).
Measure bulk 比抵抗 (resistivity) to map geology. 4-electrode arrays. Wenner (lateral), Schlumberger (depth sounding), dipole-dipole (2D imaging). Saltwater: 0.2 Ωm. 岩 (Rock): 1000+ Ωm.
Surface seismic method using first arrivals. Maps velocity layers. Requires V increasing with depth. Good for bedrock depth, water table. Equipment: geophones + seismograph. 24-96 channel arrays.

Comprehensive geotechnical investigation of a site. Includes boring, sampling, in-situ tests (標準貫入試験 (SPT), コーン貫入試験 (CPT), PS検層 (PS logging)), lab tests, and reporting. Required for all major construction in Japan.
Japanese method per 道路橋示方書 (Road Bridge Specification). Calculate CSR from 地震 (earthquake) acceleration. CRR from (N1)60cs. FL < 1.0 = liquefiable. Check at each soil layer. Typically < 20m depth.
Japanese seismic site classification per 告示1457号. 第1種地盤: 岩 (rock)/stiff (Vs30 (Vs30)>600). 第2種地盤: ordinary. 第3種地盤: soft (Vs30 (Vs30)<150). TG (site period) also used.
Standard penetration test result. N < 4: very soft/loose. N = 4-10: soft/loose. N = 10-30: medium. N = 30-50: dense. N > 50: very dense or 岩 (rock). Key parameter for Japanese foundation design.

Long-term 沈下 (settlement) of soft 粘土 (clay) under load. May take years to decades. Important in Tokyo, Osaka bay areas with thick alluvial 粘土 (clay). Monitoring with 沈下 (settlement) plates and piezometers.
Maximum load a foundation can support without shear failure. Ultimate: qu. Allowable: qa = qu/FS. FS = 3 (static). Japanese foundation design: 建築基礎構造設計指針.
Investigation using physical properties of ground. Seismic (弾性波), electrical (電気探査), electromagnetic (電磁探査), gravity (重力探査). Non-invasive subsurface mapping. License: 物理探査技士.
Uses elastic wave propagation (P波 (P-wave), S波 (S-wave)) for subsurface imaging. Methods: refraction (屈折法), reflection (反射法), 表面波探査 (MASW) (表面波), PS検層 (PS logging) (PS検層). Fundamental to 物理探査技士 exam.
Solid 岩 (rock) beneath soil overburden. 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) = 岩 (Rock) Quality Designation. 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) > 90%: excellent. 75-90%: good. 50-75%: fair. 25-50%: poor. <25%: very poor. Key for tunnel design.

Ratio of preconsolidation 応力 (stress) to current 有効応力 (effective stress). OCR = 1: normally consolidated. OCR > 1: overconsolidated (stronger). Important for 沈下 (settlement) prediction and Ko estimation.
Ambient noise (microtremors) recorded with 3-component seismometer. H/V spectral ratio peak = site resonant frequency f0. Non-invasive. Used for rapid site screening. Nakamura method (1989).

jp: ロータリーボーリング
en: ロータリーボーリング
back: Standard ground investigation method in Japan. Rotary drilling with water flush. JIS A 1220. Recovers core samples for logging. Enables 標準貫入試験 (SPT), sampling, PS検層 (PS logging), プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter) tests at depth.

Japanese national license for geological investigation. Categories: surface investigation, boring investigation, physical exploration. Required to supervise investigations for public works. Exam: written + practical.
Highest engineering qualification in Japan. Equivalent to international PE license. Geotechnical field: 応用理学部門 or 建設部門. Requires 7+ years experience + PE exam. Required for major design sign-off.

jp: モール・クーロン破壊基準
en: モール・クーロン破壊基準
back: Defines the 応力 (stress) state at failure. Failure envelope is linear in τ-σ' space. Parameters c' and φ' from drained triaxial or direct shear tests. Most fundamental equation in 土質力学 (soil mechanics).

Multichannel Analysis of Surface Waves. Measures Rayleigh wave dispersion curve from geophone array. Inverts to get Vs profile. No ボーリング孔 (borehole) needed. 24 channels, 4.5Hz geophones, 2-5m spacing typical.
Water pressure in soil voids. Hydrostatic (静水圧). Excess (過剰間隙水圧) from loading or earthquakes. Measured by piezometer (ピエゾメーター). Excess u = 0 at equilibrium (fully consolidated).

Measurement of physical properties in boreholes. PS検層 (PS logging) (弾性波検層), electrical logging, gamma-ray logging. Essential for stratigraphy correlation and geophysical parameter measurement at depth in Japan.

Waterproof optical probe lowered into ボーリング孔 (borehole) to observe wall conditions. Reveals: fracture orientation, aperture, joint infill, weathering grade, 地下水 (groundwater) inflow depth. 360° panoramic models available. Essential for tunnel and slope design in jointed 岩 (rock). Used in conjunction with core logging.

Inclination of fractures observed in ボアホールカメラ (borehole camera) footage. Measured from horizontal. Steep dip (>60°) toward tunnel face = high wedge failure risk. Camera orientation sensor allows 3D fracture orientation mapping. Critical input for kinematic stability analysis.

In-situ lateral 応力 (stress)-ひずみ (strain) test using inflatable probe in ボーリング孔 (borehole). Ménard プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter). Measures Em (プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter) 弾性係数 (modulus)) and PL (limit pressure). Em used in foundation 沈下 (settlement) calculation. PL = lateral ground failure pressure. Best for soft 岩 (rock) and stiff 粘土 (clay).

プレッシャーメーター (Pressuremeter) limit pressure — the asymptotic pressure at which soil/岩 (rock) fails laterally. Analogous to 支持力 (bearing capacity) in the lateral direction. PL correlates to undrained せん断強さ (shear strength) (cu ≈ PL/5.5) and pile lateral capacity. Key design parameter from Ménard プレッシャーメーター (pressuremeter) test.

Electromagnetic pulse radar for shallow subsurface imaging. Speed v depends on dielectric constant εr: dry 砂 (sand) v≈0.15 m/ns, wet 粘土 (clay) v≈0.07 m/ns. Depth range: 0.5m (2GHz) to ~30m (25MHz). Best in dry resistive ground. Poor in 粘土 (clay)/saltwater. Used for utilities, voids, rebar, layer boundaries.

Controls electromagnetic wave speed in soil. Air εr=1. Dry 砂 (sand) εr=4-6. Moist 砂 (sand) εr=10-15. Saturated 粘土 (clay) εr=25-40. Water εr=81. High 含水比 (water content) = slow wave = poor 地中レーダー (GPR) depth. Calibrated in field using known target depth or CMP survey.

Direct measurement of 支持力 (bearing capacity) at foundation level. Steel plate (300mm or 750mm) loaded incrementally. K30 = subgrade reaction coefficient — key for Japanese road design (≥110 MN/m³ for road base). E0 = 沈下 (settlement) 弾性係数 (modulus) from elastic theory. Scale effect: real footing larger than plate.

Ratio of applied pressure to plate 沈下 (settlement) at s=1.25mm during 平板載荷試験 (plate load test) (300mm plate). Used in Japanese road design to verify compaction. K30 = p(1.25mm) / 1.25mm × 1000. Also used for pile design and railroad track design in Japan (JIS A 1215). Values: soft 粘土 (clay) 10-30, 砂 (sand) 50-150, 礫 (gravel) >200 MN/m³.

Core-based measure of 岩 (rock) mass fracturing degree. Only natural breaks counted — mechanical drill breaks excluded. Classification: Excellent 90-100%, Good 75-90%, Fair 50-75%, Poor 25-50%, Very Poor <25%. Foundation of RMR and Q-system. Introduced by Deere (1963). Standard in Japanese tunnel and dam investigation.

Total core recovery from drilling. Different from 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) — TCR counts ALL recovered pieces including short ones. TCR=100% means perfect recovery, common in intact 岩 (rock). TCR<岩盤品質指標 (RQD) is impossible. Low TCR in soft/fractured zones indicates very poor 岩 (rock) quality or drilling problems. Both TCR and 岩盤品質指標 (RQD) are logged on ボーリング孔 (borehole) records.

Maximum investigation depth for surface methods. 表面波探査 (MASW): ~half the geophone array length. 屈折法 (Seismic refraction): ~quarter of maximum geophone offset. 地中レーダー (GPR): depends on antenna frequency and soil conductivity. Electrical 比抵抗 (resistivity): depth ~ electrode spacing (Wenner). PS検層 (PS logging): unlimited with deep boring. Matching investigation depth to target depth is key survey design skill.

Classification of 岩 (rock) mass engineering quality. RMR (岩 (Rock) Mass Rating, Bieniawski 1989): 6 parameters scored 0-100. RMR>80: Class I (very good), 61-80: Class II (good), 41-60: Class III (fair), 21-40: Class IV (poor), <21: Class V (very poor). Used directly for tunnel support design and 岩 (rock) slope analysis in Japan.

eq: Strike = direction of horizontal line on plane
back: Geometric description of a discontinuity (joint, fault, bedding). Strike = compass direction of horizontal intersection. Dip = maximum angle below horizontal. Critical for assessing instability: planar failure if dip direction = slope direction. Used with ボアホールカメラ (borehole camera) data and geological mapping. Japan: important for tunnel alignment selection.
